one_line_takeaway: A training-free, data-free black-box embedding inversion attack that per-instance fits an online ridge-regression projection to steer a frozen LLM's generation toward a target embedding, beating trained baselines cross-domain and resisting DP-style defenses.
key_finding: Zero2Text consistently outperforms all baselines on every victim model and dataset despite using no offline training data. Headline: on MS MARCO against OpenAI-3-large it reaches ROUGE-L 26.08 (~1.8x ALGEN's 14.79) and BLEU-2 16.42 (~6.4x Vec2Text-w/o-Corrector's 2.55). It is also markedly cheaper online: 0 offline queries and 2,180 online sentences / 13.88k tokens vs Vec2Text-w/Corrector's 82.60k online tokens. Its online per-instance alignment yields far higher victim-space cosine similarity (0.6063 on 3-small, 0.5447 on 3-large) than offline ALGEN (0.3229/0.2848) or TEIA (0.0039/0.0056). Standard defenses only partially help: under strong metric-LDP noise (PurMech, epsilon/d=0.25) Zero2Text still hits BLEU-1 20.36 / ROUGE-L 13.75 on PubMed, comparable to UNDEFENDED ALGEN (19.87 / 14.19), showing DP-style noise fails to neutralize the adaptive attack.
core_contribution: Zero2Text is a training-free ("zero-training") textual embedding inversion attack that recovers original text from a single target embedding under a strict black-box, data-free, cross-domain setting. Its own novelty over prior paradigms (which it explicitly contrasts in Table 1) is that it needs neither a decoder trained on leaked embedding-text pairs (as Vec2Text/GEIA direct-inversion methods do) nor an offline alignment stage using in-domain leaked pairs (as ALGEN/TEIA embedder-alignment methods do). Instead, it uses a frozen pretrained LLM (Qwen3-0.6B) as a universal token generator and performs per-instance 'recursive online alignment': at each token-generation step it queries the victim embedder with a decaying number of candidate sentences and fits a ridge-regression projection matrix W_t online (closed form W_t=(E_t^T E_t + lambda I)^-1 E_t^T E~_t) that maps the attacker's local embedding space (all-mpnet-base-v2) to the victim's space specifically for that one target embedding. Candidates are ranked by a hybrid score (Eq. 4) combining z-scored LLM logits with a confidence-weighted cosine similarity to the target, and beam search retains the best sequences.
threat_model: Strict black-box, data-free adversary. The attacker holds a target embedding vector e_v in R^d produced by an unknown victim embedder phi (e.g., extracted via a vector-DB breach or an embeddings API), and can issue a limited budget of forward API queries to phi, but has NO access to phi's parameters, gradients, or training distribution, and critically NO leaked embedding-text pairs to train any surrogate/adapter offline. The target text is assumed to come from an unknown/out-of-distribution domain (the paper's motivating gap: prior attacks implicitly assume the target domain matches their training/alignment data). Goal: reconstruct the source text x that minimizes D(phi(x_hat), e_v) within the query budget.
limitations: Not stated by the authors (the paper has no dedicated limitations section); the following are reviewer inferences from the reported results. Absolute reconstruction fidelity remains modest: best-case ROUGE-L is ~26 and BLEU-2 ~18, so full-sentence verbatim recovery is not achieved even in the strongest configuration. The attack requires interactive online query access to the live victim embedder for every target (2,180 sentences/instance on average), so it is not a purely offline breach and could be rate-limited or flagged. Generation is restricted to ASCII tokens and evaluated only on English text (MS MARCO, PubMed), leaving multilingual/non-ASCII generalization untested. Evaluation uses only 200 texts per dataset and a small 0.6B LLM generator; the defense evaluation compares only against ALGEN, not all baselines. The '1.8x' and '6.4x' headline ratios are cherry-picked pairings (ROUGE-L vs ALGEN, BLEU-2 vs the weaker Vec2Text-w/o-Corrector) rather than a single consistent comparison.

datasets_used: PubMed (biomedical abstracts, specialized domain, 200 sampled texts)
MultiHPLT English (out-of-domain corpus used to supply 1,000 offline training pairs for baselines)
quantitative_results: MS MARCO vs OpenAI-3-large: Zero2Text ROUGE-L 26.08 vs ALGEN 14.79 (~1.8x); BLEU-2 16.42 vs Vec2Text-w/o-Corrector 2.55 (~6.4x)
MS MARCO vs OpenAI-3-small: Zero2Text BLEU-1 34.44, BLEU-2 17.14, ROUGE-L 25.55, ROUGE-1 37.85, COS 0.6983
MS MARCO vs Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B: Zero2Text BLEU-1 29.29, ROUGE-L 22.55, COS 0.7323
PubMed vs OpenAI-3-small: Zero2Text BLEU-1 35.42, BLEU-2 17.97, ROUGE-L 25.64, ROUGE-1 36.39, COS 0.6859
Query cost (Table 3): Zero2Text 0 offline + 2,180 online sentences, 13.88k online tokens vs Vec2Text 82.60k online tokens; ALGEN/TEIA 1,000 offline sentences, 0 online
Alignment COS (Table 5, PubMed): Zero2Text 0.6063 (3-small) / 0.5447 (3-large) vs ALGEN 0.3229/0.2848 vs TEIA 0.0039/0.0056
Defense robustness (Table 6, PubMed): Zero2Text under PurMech epsilon/d=0.25 -> BLEU-1 20.36, ROUGE-L 13.75, ~ undefended ALGEN (19.87 / 14.19); Zero2Text undefended BLEU-1 35.42, ROUGE-L 25.64
Attacker-embedder swap to mE5-Large-Instruct (Table 4), MS MARCO vs GTR-Base: ROUGE-1 35.44, COS 0.8660
Model scalability (Fig 4): with larger LLM generator, Zero2Text ~1.6x ALGEN's ROUGE-L on MS MARCO vs OpenAI-3-large
baselines_compared: Vec2Text (Morris et al. 2023), both without and with Corrector; T5-base decoders
ALGEN (Chen et al. 2025b); Flan-T5-small decoder + few-shot cross-model alignment
